# Version 4.151.0 (Unreleased)
> **Performance and engine improvements**

## Highlights

SkiaSharp 4.151.0 brings zero-allocation hot paths across HarfBuzzSharp and SKShaper, Skia m151 engine fixes, and new ReadOnlySpan overloads for SKColor. Several hot-path allocations eliminated in text shaping (HarfBuzzSharp.Buffer.AddUtf8, Tag.Parse, SKFourByteTag.Parse, SKShaper glyph reads). The bundled Skia engine picks up upstream m151 bug fixes, and SKColor gains ReadOnlySpan<char> parsing overloads.

## Breaking Changes

*None in this preview line.*

## Engine

- **Skia m151 upstream bug fixes merged** — Two successive upstream cherry-pick passes pull in Skia chrome/m151 engine corrections, keeping the bundled renderer current with upstream stability fixes. ([#4312](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4312), [#4382](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4382))

## API Surface

- **SKColor gains ReadOnlySpan<char> parsing overloads** — SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char>, avoiding a string allocation when parsing hex colours from spans or stack-allocated buffers. ([#4345](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4345))
- **GRContextOptions no longer requires an unsafe context** — The unnecessary unsafe modifier on GRContextOptions has been removed. Projects that interact with GPU context options no longer need to enable unsafe code for this type. ([#3991](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/3991))

## Lifecycle & Internals

- **HarfBuzzSharp hot paths made allocation-free** — Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled encoder instead of allocating per call, and Tag.Parse no longer allocates a char[4] on every invocation — both reduce GC pressure in tight text-shaping loops. ([#4376](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4376), [#4408](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4408))
- **SKShaper glyph reads use zero-copy spans** — Glyph result data from the native shaper is now read directly via spans rather than intermediate copies, reducing per-run allocations in shaping pipelines. ([#4388](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4388))
- **SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocates per call** — Eliminates a per-call char[4] heap allocation in SKFourByteTag.Parse, matching the same fix applied to the HarfBuzz Tag type. ([#4396](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4396))
